headline: LG Electronics, Nvidia in talks over robots, AI data centres and mobility
summary: South Korean ‌tech company ‌LG Electronics said on ​Wednesday that it and Nvidia have been discussing ‌cooperation ⁠in robotics, AI data centres ⁠and mobility. The comments came ​after Madison ​Huang, ​a senior ‌director for physical AI platforms at Nvidia, and the daughter of ‌its CEO, ​visited ​LG ​Electronics and ‌other South Korean ​companies, ​according to reports in Korean ​media. (Reporting ‌by Hyunjoo Jin; ​Editing by ​Andrew Heavens)

headline: Exclusive-Big Chinese tech firms scramble to secure Huawei AI chips after DeepSeek V4 launch, sources say
summary: Demand for Huawei's Ascend 950 AI chips has surged following the release of DeepSeek's V4 artificial intelligence model that runs on the Shenzhen-based tech firm's chips, with major Chinese internet firms rushing to secure orders, three people familiar with the matter said.  China's biggest internet firms ‌including ByteDance, Tencent and Alibaba are reaching out to Huawei about new chip orders, said the sources, who are familiar with the procurement discussions.  While the 950PR significantly outperforms Nvidia's H20 chip - the most ​powerful chip Nvidia was permitted to sell in China until Beijing blocked its import last year - it still trails the American firm's H200, a more advanced processor that has been caught up in regulatory limbo.

content.title: Exclusive-Big Chinese tech firms scramble to secure Huawei AI chips after DeepSeek V4 launch, sources say
content.summary: Demand for Huawei's Ascend 950 AI chips has surged following the release of DeepSeek's V4 artificial intelligence model that runs on the Shenzhen-based tech firm's chips, with major Chinese internet firms rushing to secure orders, three people familiar with the matter said.  China's biggest internet firms ‌including ByteDance, Tencent and Alibaba are reaching out to Huawei about new chip orders, said the sources, who are familiar with the procurement discussions.  While the 950PR significantly outperforms Nvidia's H20 chip - the most ​powerful chip Nvidia was permitted to sell in China until Beijing blocked its import last year - it still trails the American firm's H200, a more advanced processor that has been caught up in regulatory limbo.
